Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Elcarim Science Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Elcarim Science Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore on 2012-08-07 and was involved in scientific research and development, as well as the supply of products within the security industry in Singapore and overseas
Three individuals, including Zhang's wife, were placed on Elcarim's payroll and were paid a total of $86,255 in salaries, bonuses, and CPF contributions
Elcarim's OCBC account received a deposit of $60,000 from Zhang's ANZ loan proceeds on 2013-07-23
The Ministry of Manpower made inquiries regarding the company's failure to pay CPF contributions for certain employees

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director contravened management obligations by failing to secure export permits, utilizing company funds for personal loan repayments, and placing non-working individuals on the corporate payroll
Party_Details.Rule: A director is legally responsible for acting in the company's best interests and must account for any unauthorized use of corporate funds or failure to comply with statutory licensing requirements
Party_Details.Application: The director secretly hired 'phantom' employees and diverted funds for personal debt. the hires were authorized for business development and the loan was taken out for the company's cash-flow benefit
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court allowed the claim regarding the three employees, finding they did no work. However, it dismissed the claims for the ANZ loan and SGCA permits due to insufficient evidence of the director's personal culpability or proof that the funds were not used for company benefit.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Zhang Yongtai

Facts.Text: Zhang Yongtai served as the sole director of Elcarim from its incorporation, responsible for its day-to-day management
The director deposited $60,000 from a personal ANZ bank loan into the company's OCBC account to assist with cash flow issues
The director transferred half of the company's shares to another individual on the instructions of the primary shareholder
A Letter of Appointment was signed designating the director as the company's general manager

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Zhang was responsible for the SGCA charges, the ANZ loan repayments, and the payments to the three employees
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act bona fide and in good faith in the interests of the company and use reasonable diligence in discharging his duties
Party_Details.Application: Dou instructed him to hire the Three Employees to increase Singaporean headcount so that Elcarim could hire more Chinese employees. Elcarim's case was that the Three Employees did not do any work for Elcarim
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court determined that the director was legally responsible for the salaries paid to the three individuals as there was no evidence they performed any work. The director was found not liable for the SGCA charges or the ANZ loan repayments.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Zhang Yongtai

Facts.Text: The company terminated the director's employment with immediate effect without providing any notice period
A purported share pledge agreement between Zhang and Dou concerned Zhang's shares in Elcarim
Dou caused Zhang's shares in Elcarim to be transferred to himself
The financial statement for financial year 2012 to 2013 recorded a loan owed to a director in the sum of $685,539

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a former director is entitled to damages for wrongful termination of employment and the disputed transfer of corporate shares under an allegedly forged agreement
Party_Details.Rule: A party seeking damages for the loss of shares in conversion must prove the transfer was unauthorized, and an employee is entitled to reasonable notice or salary in lieu of notice upon termination
Party_Details.Application: His signature on the share transfer documents was forged and he was entitled to three months' notice. the transfer was a valid exercise of rights under the SPA and the employment contract was fabricated
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court allowed the claim for wrongful termination, awarding one month's salary. Crucially, the court found the director had not signed the SPA or transfer documents and awarded damages for the loss of the shares to be assessed.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Elcarim Science Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: The company terminated Zhang's employment without notice on 10 June 2015
The company paid Zhang $11,000 as salary for May 2015

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company is legally responsible for unpaid salary and notice pay where the validity of the underlying employment agreement is disputed
Party_Details.Rule: In the absence of a valid written contract, an employee is entitled to a reasonable notice period based on their seniority and length of service
Party_Details.Application: The Plaintiff's salary was only $11,000 and the provided written contract was not genuine. he was entitled to $18,000 a month and three months' notice
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found the written contract unenforceable but determined that a one-month notice period was reasonable given the director's position, awarding him $11,000 in damages.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Dou Suoke

Facts.Text: Dou contributed capital to Elcarim from its incorporation on 2012-08-07, and Zhang held half of the shares on trust for him
A Share Pledge Agreement dated 2015-02-28 was purportedly signed by Zhang in the presence of a witness at a Singapore apartment
On 2015-06-10, Dou caused Zhang's 50% shareholding in Elcarim to be transferred to himself

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the transfer of Zhang's shares to Dou was authorized under the share pledge agreement
Party_Details.Application: Dou's position was that the share transfer was lawful because it was made pursuant to the share pledge agreement between him and Zhang. Zhang disputed the validity of the share transfer
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court held that the shareholder improperly transferred the shares. Based on factual inconsistencies in the witness testimony and handwriting analysis, the court found the director did not sign the documents and held the shareholder liable for damages.
